target.title: fix(acp): treat missing cwd as stale bound session
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: bound ACP sessions stayed attached after the runtime `cwd` disappeared, so retries kept failing against a dead session.
- Why it matters: affected threads remain wedged until someone manually clears the stale binding.
- What changed: treat missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors as stale-session signals in both dispatch-time cleanup and reset-in-place cleanup, and cover both paths with regressions.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stale cleanup still only triggers for `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`; this does not broaden cleanup to generic turn failures.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #60720
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: stale-session detection only recognized metadata and resource-not-found init failures, so a missing ACP runtime working directory did not trigger stale unbind.
- Missing detection / guardrail: reset-in-place failure handling returned the error without clearing the stale binding, and no regression covered missing-`cwd` init failures.
- Contributing context (if known): the old fix branch had drifted too far from current `main`, but the bug still reproduced on April 18, 2026.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`, `src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors unbind stale bindings during both dispatch and reset-in-place recovery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: both failure paths are local runtime/control-plane seams and do not need a live ACP backend to prove the cleanup behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Bound ACP conversations recover automatically when the runtime working directory is gone instead of staying wedged behind a stale binding.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[bound ACP turn/reset] -> [missing cwd init failure] -> [binding remains] -> [next attempt fails again]

After:
[bound ACP turn/reset] -> [missing cwd init failure] -> [stale binding removed] -> [next attempt can recreate]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: NixOS Linux
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22.21.1, pnpm 11.1.0, OpenClaw package 2026.5.12-beta.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP persistent bindings
- Relevant config (redacted): session-bound ACP conversation with runtime `cwd` configured

### Steps

1. Reproduce an `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` error whose message is `ACP runtime working directory does not exist: ...` on a bound ACP session.
2. Trigger either dispatch-time reply handling or `resetAcpSessionInPlace` for that session.
3. Observe whether the session binding is removed.

### Expected

- The stale binding is removed and the failure is returned as skipped/recoverable cleanup, so the next attempt can recreate state.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale binding remained attached and the same failure repeated.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reproduced the missing-`cwd` classification gap against current `main`; ran targeted ACP dispatch/reset tests after the fix.
- Edge cases checked: stale-binding cleanup failure still returns the original skipped error instead of masking it.
- What you did **not** verify: live ACP backend behavior outside the focused test harness.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: stale-session cleanup could be triggered too broadly.
  - Mitigation: the shared helper still requires `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, and the new coverage locks the intended missing-`cwd` path specifically.

## AI Assistance

- AI was used to reproduce the current bug on `main`, implement the fix, run targeted tests, and draft this PR body.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A bound ACP session whose persisted runtime `cwd` no longer exists should be treated as stale, unbind the affected conversation, and return recoverable/skipped cleanup instead of keeping the stale binding attached.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on NixOS Linux; Node.js v22.21.1; pnpm 11.1.0; OpenClaw package version 2026.5.12-beta.1; base `origin/main` at `9eed27a9b8`; branch `fix/acp-stale-cwd-replacement` at `66007fdd20`. This used the actual OpenClaw repo test runner and source tree.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts -t resetAcpSessionInPlace
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
timeout -k5s 300s codex review --base origin/main
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  41 passed (41)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.42s

$ pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts -t resetAcpSessionInPlace
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed | 4 skipped (11)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 4.10s

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 25ms on 5 files using 1 threads.

$ timeout -k5s 300s codex review --base origin/main
The change consistently extends stale ACP session detection to missing working directories and unbinds affected session bindings in both dispatch and configured-binding reset paths. I did not find a discrete correctness issue introduced by the diff.
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The missing-cwd dispatch path calls `unbind({ targetSessionKey, reason: "acp-session-init-failed" })`; the reset path handles both thrown missing-cwd errors and returned `runtimeNotice` missing-cwd errors by unbinding stale bindings and returning `{ ok: false, skipped: true, error: ... }`.
- **What was not tested**: Full live ACP backend recovery outside this local OpenClaw checkout was not exercised; the proof is focused on the implicated OpenClaw dispatch/reset seams.

target.title: feat(plugins): accept slot owner records
target.body:
## Summary

This PR implements the read-side normalization layer proposed in #70823: `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` can now be read as either a bare plugin id string or an object-form ownership record with an `owner` field.

What problem does this PR solve?

- Today, OpenClaw slot ownership is represented only as a bare string such as `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"`.
- That makes ownership checks brittle: every runtime, doctor, validation, install/update, and plugin trust path has to assume the raw slot value is a string.
- It also blocks the next step in #70823: recording slot ownership provenance without breaking existing configs.

What changes?

- Adds `PluginSlotOwnerRecord` and `PluginSlotValue` config types.
- Extends the config schema so `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` accept either `"plugin-id"` or `{ "owner": "plugin-id", ...metadata }`.
- Adds `resolvePluginSlotOwner(value)` as the shared read-side helper for slot owner normalization.
- Updates slot consumers across config normalization, validation, stale config scanning, doctor repair paths, startup plugin preloading, active runtime/status paths, context-engine resolution, memory dreaming, provider auth alias/env-var trust, uninstall cleanup, and plugin ID migration.
- Preserves object-form metadata when plugin ID migration changes only the `owner` field.
- Updates docs and focused regression coverage for object-form slot values.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No write-side canonicalization. OpenClaw does not start writing `{ owner, claimed_at, claimed_by_version }` records in this PR.
- No overwrite invariant or `--force` behavior. Silent overwrite prevention remains a follow-up to #70823.
- No new slot keys. This PR only covers the existing `memory` and `contextEngine` slots.
- No direct implementation of the memory role-slot architecture from #86210.

Intended outcome:

- Existing configs keep working unchanged.
- New object-form slot records are accepted everywhere relevant on read.
- Later PRs can extend this groundwork instead of reimplementing slot-owner parsing in each surface.

## Linked context

Closes #70823.

Directly related work:

- #86210: adds the multi-slot memory role architecture requested by #60572. This PR intentionally lands the smaller owner-record primitive first, or can be rebased after #86210 by extending the same helper/schema pattern across the new memory role slots.
- #83637: adds per-agent compaction/context-pruning overrides. This PR is complementary because it only changes global plugin-slot value shape and keeps context-engine/compaction semantics separate.
- #60572: user demand for composable memory slots. This PR is supporting groundwork for #60572 by creating the owner-normalization primitive that the role-slot architecture can reuse.

Supporting related PRs and issues:

- #57507: prior multi-kind plugin work showed that one plugin may legitimately participate in multiple exclusive plugin categories.
- #64192: context-engine slot normalization precedent.
- #75640: plugin install slot-selection scope precedent.
- #81512 and #81515: stale plugin reference cleanup and doctor/reporting precedent for slot references.
- #82438: memory-slot warning precedent.
- #85060: memory provider parity proof pattern for active memory plugin behavior.
- #86591: current external-PR body/proof structure used as a template for ClawSweeper-friendly real behavior proof.

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, slot readers commonly assumed `plugins.slots.<slot>` was a string. That assumption is exactly what #70823 needs to retire.

The problem is not just schema shape. If only the schema accepts object-form records, but runtime paths keep reading raw values directly, then object-form configs fail in subtle places:

- startup preloading may skip the selected plugin;
- doctor and stale-config scans may miss or misreport owners;
- context-engine resolution may fall back to `legacy`;
- memory status/tool-disable paths may misread `none`;
- provider auth alias/env-var trust may ignore a selected workspace context engine;
- plugin update can collapse or lose ownership metadata.

This PR makes the read-side contract real by routing those consumers through one shared helper.

## What Changed

- `src/config/types.plugins.ts`
  - Adds `PluginSlotOwnerRecord`.
  - Adds `PluginSlotValue = string | PluginSlotOwnerRecord`.
  - Applies the value type to `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine`.

- `src/config/zod-schema.ts`
  - Accepts string slot values and object values with required `owner`.
  - Allows additional object fields so future provenance fields can be carried without another schema turn.

- `src/plugins/slots.ts`
  - Adds `resolvePluginSlotOwner(value)`.
  - Normalizes strings and object-form owners to one scalar owner id.
  - Treats blank values as unset and `none` as the slot-disabled sentinel.
  - Uses the helper in exclusive slot switching so existing object-form ownership is recognized.

- Runtime/config consumers now normalize slot owners before comparison:
  - config normalization
  - config validation
  - stale plugin config detection
  - doctor route and context-engine compatibility checks
  - gateway startup plugin id selection
  - context-engine resolution
  - memory dreaming config resolution
  - memory status and tool-disable logic
  - provider auth alias/env-var workspace trust checks
  - uninstall cleanup
  - plugin update ID migration

- Documentation now states that slot values may be string ids or `{ owner: "<plugin-id>" }` records.

## Merge-Order Compatibility

This PR is deliberately a groundwork PR, not a competing architecture.

If this lands before #86210:

- #86210 should extend `PluginSlotsConfig`/schema with `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` using the same `PluginSlotValue` shape.
- #86210 should use `resolvePluginSlotOwner()` at new role-slot read sites instead of adding new local normalization.
- Expected rebase work should be ordinary schema/docs/test conflicts, not a design rewrite.

If #86210 lands before this:

- This branch should rebase by applying the object-form slot value support to the new memory role keys as well as existing `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- The core helper remains valid; it just gets called from more role-slot consumers.

If #83637 lands before or after this:

- The two changes should stay complementary.
- #83637 controls which agent scope supplies compaction/context-pruning settings.
- This PR controls how global plugin-slot owners are represented and normalized on read.
- Later compaction/context-engine work should consume normalized `contextEngine` ownership where it reads that slot, without changing per-agent compaction semantics.

Follow-up intended after this PR:

- Write-side canonicalization to produce object-form owner records.
- Slot overwrite invariants and explicit force/override behavior.
- Plugin-side adoption of the normalized owner abstraction.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: object-form `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` records should behave the same as bare string slot owners across config parsing, validation, doctor checks, startup planning, runtime memory/context-engine resolution, provider auth trust checks, plugin lifecycle cleanup, and plugin ID migration.
- Real environment tested: Dev-01 source checkout at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/workspace/repos/openclaw`; branch `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records`; head `27585fb987df8d6c68886566a6280e755a507456`; local Node/Vitest/OpenClaw repo toolchain.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
npx vitest run src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/context-engine-host-compat.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts src/secrets/provider-env-vars.dynamic.test.ts
npx vitest run src/plugins/update.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
npx oxfmt --check <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files 11 passed (11)
Tests 301 passed (301)

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 80 passed (80)

OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
[base-config-schema] ok

All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check passed
tsgo core check exitCode=0
```

- Observed result after fix: object-form slot owners are accepted by schema, normalize to the same owner ids as string slots, preserve `none` behavior, participate in context-engine and memory runtime resolution, validate missing plugin references correctly, trust selected workspace context-engine plugins for provider auth only when the normalized owner matches, reset/update selected slots during uninstall/update, and preserve extra object metadata when plugin ID migration only changes `owner`.
- What was not tested: live production gateway behavior, write-side object canonicalization, overwrite enforcement, or future #86210 memory role slots. Those are out of scope for this read-side normalization PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a config/runtime parsing and normalization groundwork PR, so the proof is focused local deterministic execution rather than a live model/channel proof. No production config, gateway restart, external provider account, or live plugin registry state was mutated.
- Before evidence: #70823 documents the pre-change limitation: slot ownership was only machine-readable through direct string comparison, leaving no durable place for ownership provenance and forcing each consumer/plugin to repeat brittle raw-slot checks.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
npx vitest run src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/context-engine-host-compat.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts src/secrets/provider-env-vars.dynamic.test.ts
npx vitest run src/plugins/update.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Slot normalization for string owners, object owners, blank values, and `none`.
- Config schema acceptance for object-form `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- Config validation diagnostics for missing plugin references inside object-form slot owners.
- Exclusive slot selection when the previous owner is object-form.
- Context-engine resolution for object-form `contextEngine`.
- Doctor host compatibility and route-warning paths with object-form context-engine owners.
- Memory status/tool-disable/dreaming paths with object-form `memory`.
- Provider auth alias/env-var trust checks for selected workspace context engines represented as owner records.
- Plugin update migration preserving object-form metadata while changing the `owner`.

## Compatibility and Risk

Backward compatibility:

- Existing string slot configs remain valid.
- Existing `"none"` memory-slot behavior remains valid.
- Existing default behavior remains unchanged: memory defaults to `memory-core`; context engine defaults to `legacy`.
- Existing write paths that still write strings remain valid.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- Config schema now accepts object records for existing slot values.
- Consumers that previously compared raw slot strings now compare normalized owners.
- Plugin update migration now preserves object-form slot metadata where possible.

Primary risks:

- A direct raw-slot read could remain somewhere outside the audited surfaces.
- A future #86210 rebase could add new role-slot read sites that need the same helper.
- Accepting passthrough metadata on slot records is intentionally forward-compatible, but reviewers may want to confirm the object shape is the right minimum for #70823.

Risk mitigation:

- The branch adds one shared resolver rather than multiple ad hoc conversions.
- Focused tests cover schema, normalization, runtime consumers, doctor paths, provider auth trust, status/tool behavior, and lifecycle migration.
- This PR avoids write-side canonicalization/enforcement, keeping the blast radius to read compatibility.

## Current Branch State

- Base branch: `main`
- Head branch: `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records`
- Current head: `27585fb987df8d6c68886566a6280e755a507456`
- Commit: `feat(plugins): accept slot owner records`
- Worktree at draft time: clean, branch ahead of `origin/main` by 1
- Push/PR creation: not done yet

## Current Review State

Next action:

- Review this PR body draft.
- Push `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records` after approval.
- Open the PR against `openclaw/openclaw:main`.

Still waiting on:

- Schwi approval of the PR body.
- Push authorization.
- GitHub Actions after PR creation.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Refreshed final end-to-end proof for head `7c0b78f1b36890b6348242324f71577d2291f8a0`, including the post-proof legacy `registerMemoryPromptSection` fallback repair, granular memory role slots, legacy `plugins.slots.memory` upgrade handling, per-agent memory-only overrides, LosslessClaw as global `contextEngine`, Honcho/QMD-backed recall paths, dreaming/compaction/userModel/capture role activation, wiki/public artifact handling, disabled recall, and loader/cache-sensitive per-agent slot behavior.
- **Real environment tested:** Dev-01 source checkout at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/workspace/repos/openclaw`; Orion/Nano-01 proof checkout at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210`; Node `v24.15.0`; `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`; self-hosted Honcho at `127.0.0.1:8000`; LosslessClaw configured as `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; redacted artifacts under `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210/.artifacts/proof-refresh-7c0b78f1-20260525T221705Z`.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix:** Rolled Orion back to old base `0f35ec29d34989a8e957cbc59607b261cf5d6695`, before this PR's slot schema/code; stripped granular `memory.*` slot keys so old OpenClaw saw legacy `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"`; ran old `config validate` and `doctor`; upgraded the same config home to PR head; ran `doctor --fix` and `config validate`; ran focused memory/plugin/config/doctor/runtime/CLI prompt Vitest suites; ran the agent shard; ran lint, type, diff, direct model, live model, live gateway prompt, Honcho status/ask, deterministic five-plugin role routing, wiki artifact, legacy prompt fallback, and five live agent prompt proofs.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted runtime logs and JSON artifacts are indexed at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210/.artifacts/proof-refresh-7c0b78f1-20260525T221705Z/redacted/99-final-refresh-summary.json`; copied live output markers include `OPENCLAW_PR86210_7C0B78F1_OK`, `LOCAL_HONCHO_7C0B78F1_OK`, `LEGACY_PROMPT_FALLBACK_OK`, and `AGENT_*_7C0B78F1_OK` for all five proof agents. Key proof files include `redacted/13-upgraded-config-summary.json`, `redacted/40-memory-slot-proof.json`, `redacted/41-wiki-artifact-proof.json`, `redacted/42-legacy-prompt-fallback-proof.json`, and `redacted/50-agent-*.json`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Legacy config upgraded cleanly, canonical `memory.recall` was added while legacy `memory` remained compatible, role-slot routing selected the expected plugins per agent, disabled recall suppressed recall artifacts/prompt fallback, wiki artifacts followed active recall, Honcho/QMD and LosslessClaw paths initialized, and live direct/gateway model plus five configured agent prompts returned the expected markers.
- **Upgrade evidence:** Old stripped config validated on pre-PR code, then PR `doctor --fix` copied legacy `plugins.slots.memory` to canonical `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` while retaining the legacy key for compatibility. Artifacts: `redacted/04-old-stripped-config-summary.json`, `redacted/05-old-config-validate-rerun.log`, `redacted/06-old-doctor-preview-rerun.log`, `redacted/11-pr-doctor-fix.log`, `redacted/12-pr-config-validate-after.log`, `redacted/13-upgraded-config-summary.json`.
- **Deterministic evidence:** Focused PR suite passed, including `memory-state`, `memory-runtime`, `loader`, `memory-wiki`, hooks/skills, effective plugin IDs, uninstall/update/channel planning, doctor legacy migration/stale config, slots, and `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` for legacy prompt compatibility. Sequential lint shards passed after Orion killed the parallel `pnpm lint` process under memory pressure: `22a-lint-scripts`, `22b-lint-core`, `22c-lint-extensions`; `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json`, `tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`, and `git diff --check` passed.
- **Role-slot evidence:** Five proof memory plugins loaded: `proof-core`, `proof-honcho`, `proof-capture`, `proof-dream`, and `proof-compact`. Routing result: `main -> proof-core`, `honcho-agent -> proof-honcho`, `dreamer-agent -> proof-core`, `disabled-recall-agent -> <none>`, and `split-agent -> proof-core`; compaction routed through `proof-compact-provider`; capture, dreaming, userModel, recall, and compaction activation metadata were recorded. Artifacts: `redacted/40-memory-slot-proof.json`, `redacted/41-wiki-artifact-proof.json`.
- **Legacy prompt fallback evidence:** New proof `redacted/42-legacy-prompt-fallback-proof.json` shows config-aware prompt building falls back to the legacy `legacy-memory-v1` registration when `memory.recall` selects a plugin without a capability, and still returns no prompt/capability when `memory.recall = "none"`.
- **Live evidence:** Direct model proof returned `OPENCLAW_PR86210_7C0B78F1_OK`; broad live direct model suite passed against stripped model config; live gateway prompt/tool/image path completed for `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`; Honcho status/ask passed and returned `LOCAL_HONCHO_7C0B78F1_OK`; five configured live agents returned exact markers: `AGENT_main_7C0B78F1_OK`, `AGENT_honcho-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`, `AGENT_dreamer-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`, `AGENT_disabled-recall-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`, and `AGENT_split-agent_7C0B78F1_OK`. Artifacts: `redacted/30-live-config-summary.json`, `redacted/30b-broad-model-config-summary.json`, `redacted/32-models-status.json`, `redacted/33-model-run.json`, `redacted/34c-live-models-broad-model-home.log`, `redacted/35c-live-gateway-models-broad-model-home-timeout300.log`, `redacted/36-honcho-status.log`, `redacted/37-honcho-ask.log`, `redacted/50-agent-*.json`.
- **Known unrelated Orion baselines:** `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` fails on both PR head and upstream-main old base in the Orion checkout; the gateway live suite's static auth-sanitizer assertion also fails on upstream-main. Both comparisons are captured as `redacted/21b-code-mode-rerun.log`, `redacted/21c-code-mode-upstream-main.log`, and `redacted/35d-gateway-auth-sanitizer-upstream-main.log`. The PR-relevant CLI prompt, memory routing, direct model, live gateway prompt/tool/image, Honcho, wiki, and five-agent proofs passed.
- **Final artifact index:** `redacted/99-final-refresh-summary.json`.
- **What was not tested:** Hosted `api.honcho.dev` was not used; Honcho proof is self-hosted/local on Orion. The future owner-provenance object shape from #70823 remains out of scope.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current pushed head:

- `788fef03e308b954c9322d5b5d8f9f35024493a5` (`fix(config): hide internal config rollback symbol`)

Local/Orion gates from the refreshed head:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` on old base and PR head: passed.
- `corepack pnpm build:strict-smoke` on old base and PR head: passed.
- Old-base stripped config `config validate` and `doctor`: passed.
- PR-head upgraded config `doctor`, `doctor --fix`, and `config validate`: passed.
- Focused memory/plugin/config/doctor/runtime Vitest suite: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json`: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- Five-agent memory role live prompt proof: passed.
- Direct live model proof: passed.
- Live model suite: 17 tests passed.
- Live gateway model suite: 60 tests passed.
- Self-hosted Honcho status/ask proof: passed.
- LosslessClaw context-engine startup/migration proof: captured in redacted live logs.

GitHub Actions for pushed head `788fef03e308b954c9322d5b5d8f9f35024493a5` are expected to run after this body refresh.

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` overrides so each configured agent can resolve maintenance/runtime behavior from its active agent scope instead of always inheriting every value from `agents.defaults`.

This is for multi-agent installations where main, channel/proxy, local/self-hosted, and Codex/app-server agents need different compaction, pruning, timeout, thinking-level, and transcript-maintenance behavior.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related: #52751, #69988, #19501, #15826, #43834, #6571, #6572, #30452, #57901, #58077, #59863, #60512, #80784, #82008

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, per-agent compaction settings were either rejected by schema or only partially honored. Several runtime paths still read `agents.defaults.compaction` / `agents.defaults.contextPruning` even when the active `agents.list[]` entry had its own settings.

That weakened these setups:

- main/default agent compacts with one model/provider while another agent compacts with another
- local/self-hosted agents use local provider aliases for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit or ignore OpenClaw model overrides and keep native server-side compaction behavior
- legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys still need an explicit non-main `agentId` preserved across queued/direct compaction and maintenance paths
- queued/CLI/context-engine compaction should use `compaction.thinkingLevel`, not the active run's normal thinking level

## What Changed

- Adds strict schema/docs support for `agents.list[].compaction` and per-agent `contextPruning`.
- Deep-merges per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` blocks over `agents.defaults`, so partial overrides inherit unspecified fields.
- Resolves compaction model/provider/auth-profile/timeout/cache/pruning/reserve/thinking settings through the active session agent.
- Threads `sessionAgentId` through queued/direct compaction, CLI compaction, context-engine maintenance, Codex app-server lifecycle, embedded cache-TTL finalization, memory flush, transcript rotation, and follow-up/notice paths.
- Keeps explicit OpenClaw `compaction.model` behavior separate from Codex/app-server native runtime selection.
- Adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent scope in direct, queued, CLI, Codex lifecycle, cache-TTL, memory-flush, empty/partial merge, and compaction thinking-level propagation paths.

## Reviewer Notes

The key invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR explicitly passes `agentId` into compaction and maintenance params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: redacted real multi-agent OpenClaw runtime proof showing different agents loaded with different per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` settings, plus an actual live Codex app-server compaction run that correctly refused to treat OpenClaw model overrides as Codex server-side compaction model selection.
- Real environment tested: live hot-patched OpenClaw gateway on `Dev-01`, `OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (054e2f8)`, Node `v24.15.0`, PR branch `feat/agent-compaction-overrides`, head `04170e48eb01fc29f7f3acb1a830c5742b41c3ea`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
openclaw config validate
openclaw gateway call config.get
openclaw sessions --all-agents --active 120 --json
journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway.service --since '2026-05-23 21:50:00 UTC'
```

- Evidence after fix: live gateway config accepted three Discord-bound proof agents with different per-agent override combinations:

```json
{
  "clawsweeper-proof-flat": {
    "channel": "1507860722950864939",
    "compaction": {
      "mode": "default",
      "model": "openai/gpt-5.4-mini",
      "thinkingLevel": "off",
      "reserveTokens": 2048,
      "keepRecentTokens": 1024,
      "maxHistoryShare": 0.25,
      "identifierPolicy": "off",
      "recentTurnsPreserve": 1,
      "postIndexSync": "off",
      "postCompactionSections": [],
      "timeoutSeconds": 60,
      "notifyUser": true
    },
    "contextPruning": {
      "mode": "cache-ttl",
      "ttl": "1m",
      "keepLastAssistants": 1,
      "minPrunableToolChars": 512,
      "tools": { "allow": ["exec_command", "web_search"] }
    }
  },
  "clawsweeper-proof-nested": {
    "channel": "1507860803968172062",
    "compaction": {
      "mode": "safeguard",
      "model": "github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini",
      "thinkingLevel": "off",
      "identifierPolicy": "custom",
      "qualityGuard": { "enabled": true, "maxRetries": 1 },
      "midTurnPrecheck": { "enabled": false },
      "memoryFlush": {
        "enabled": true,
        "model": "github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini",
        "softThresholdTokens": 128,
        "forceFlushTranscriptBytes": "32kb"
      },
      "postIndexSync": "await",
      "timeoutSeconds": 120,
      "notifyUser": true
    },
    "contextPruning": {
      "mode": "cache-ttl",
      "ttl": "30s",
      "keepLastAssistants": 0,
      "minPrunableToolChars": 256,
      "tools": { "deny": ["browser"] },
      "hardClear": { "enabled": true }
    }
  },
  "clawsweeper-proof-transcript": {
    "channel": "1507860850998771823",
    "compaction": {
      "mode": "safeguard",
      "model": "openai/gpt-5.4-mini",
      "thinkingLevel": "minimal",
      "qualityGuard": { "enabled": false },
      "midTurnPrecheck": { "enabled": true },
      "memoryFlush": { "enabled": false },
      "truncateAfterCompaction": true,
      "maxActiveTranscriptBytes": "16kb",
      "keepRecentTokens": 512,
      "recentTurnsPreserve": 0,
      "postIndexSync": "async",
      "timeoutSeconds": 90,
      "notifyUser": false
    },
    "contextPruning": {
      "mode": "cache-ttl",
      "ttl": "10s",
      "keepLastAssistants": 0,
      "minPrunableToolChars": 128,
      "hardClear": { "enabled": true }
    }
  }
}
```

- Evidence after fix: live sessions were created under the distinct agent scopes and channel bindings:

```text
agent:clawsweeper-proof-flat:discord:channel:1507860722950864939
  sessionId=36195e24-d3c0-4b94-b673-0699a597a33f
  provider=openai-codex model=gpt-5.5 contextTokens=272000 totalTokens=59605 compactionCount=1

agent:clawsweeper-proof-nested:discord:channel:1507860803968172062
  sessionId=72060c7e-f8a3-447f-92fe-de14206f5555
  provider=openai-codex model=gpt-5.5 contextTokens=272000 totalTokens=121538

  sessionId=ddf6b2a9-95d6-4b8f-be92-6a7731f488c3
  provider=openai-codex model=gpt-5.5 contextTokens=272000 totalTokens=66218
```

- Evidence after fix: manual `/compact` on the first proof agent completed through the native Codex app-server compaction path:

```text
2026-05-23T22:03:21.814+00:00 message dispatch started: sessionKey=agent:clawsweeper-proof-flat:discord:channel:1507860722950864939
2026-05-23T22:03:24.567+00:00 ignoring OpenClaw compaction overrides for Codex app-server compaction; Codex uses native server-side compaction
2026-05-23T22:03:26.549+00:00 started codex app-server compaction
2026-05-23T22:03:39.350+00:00 completed codex app-server compaction
```

- Observed result after fix: the live gateway accepted and ran three distinct per-agent config shapes, routed messages to the expected agent/channel scopes, and recorded a real manual compaction on `clawsweeper-proof-flat`. The Codex app-server runtime correctly logged that OpenClaw compaction model overrides are ignored for Codex-native compaction, preventing the PR body from overclaiming model/provider override behavior for Codex app-server sessions.
- What was not tested: the live Codex app-server sessions do not prove that OpenClaw-selected compaction models were used, because Codex owns native server-side compaction. Direct OpenClaw compaction model/provider selection is covered by the source/runtime regression tests below.

## Validation

Acceptance commands requested by ClawSweeper:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
```

Result:

```text

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 25 passed (25)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files 4 passed (4)
Tests 115 passed (115)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 19.18s
```

Schema baseline:

```text

[base-config-schema] ok
```

Config docs baseline:

```text

OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
```

Note: this docs-baseline check initially caught stale committed hash drift. I regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, committed it as `04170e48eb01fc29f7f3acb1a830c5742b41c3ea`, and reran the check successfully.

Additional gate:

```text
git diff --check
passed
```

## Current Branch State

- Base branch: `main`
- Head: `04170e48eb01fc29f7f3acb1a830c5742b41c3ea`
- GitHub reports the PR as mergeable; checks are still running after the latest push.

target.title: fix(memory): reject prompt-like memory stores
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens the memory LanceDB plugin so explicit `memory_store` calls reject prompt-like instruction payloads before they reach embeddings or persistent storage.

## Changes

- Reuses the existing `looksLikePromptInjection(text)` heuristic in the explicit `memory_store` tool path.
- Returns a rejected tool result with `reason: "prompt_injection_detected"` before embedding, duplicate search, or LanceDB writes.
- Adds regression coverage for the rejected path and for an ordinary preference memory still storing normally.

## Validation

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Notes

Internal tracking: #692. No `CHANGELOG.md` update.

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: explicit memory stores that look like prompt instructions are rejected before embedding or storage, while normal preference memories still store.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on Node v22.22.2 using the memory LanceDB plugin test harness.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: the targeted test file passed with 47 tests, including the new rejection and accepted-storage regression.

target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** DeepInfra plugin: (1) discovers the full live chat catalog (88 models, vs 7 hardcoded on `main`) — wizard-vs-discovery guard no longer suppresses dynamic results; (2) registers live `image-gen` and `video-gen` catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern); (3) injects Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers on outgoing payloads for `deepinfra/anthropic/*` requests (OpenRouter wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"` before this patch); (4) wires TTS / STT / image-gen / VLM end-to-end through the deepinfra speech, media-understanding, image-gen, and VLM providers. **Real environment tested:** Hosted Lightsail VM (Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0), openclaw `2026.5.21` built from this PR, `openclaw.service` running as a systemd unit. Live network to `api.deepinfra.com`. DeepInfra key configured. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Capability inventory commands (chat catalog + image + audio providers): ```bash pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` Then in the webchat UI: image-gen → VLM → STT (inbound audio attachment auto-transcribed) → TTS in one chat session (full session log attached below). Anthropic cache markers — HTTP-level capture via a small Node logging proxy in front of `api.deepinfra.com`; agent fires one `anthropic/*` turn, proxy records the outgoing body: ```bash openclaw agent --local --model deepinfra/anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 \ --message "Say hello briefly." < /dev/null cat /tmp/cache-proxy.log ``` Test suite: ```bash pnpm test extensions/deepinfra pnpm check:changed ``` **Evidence after fix:** Terminal — `capability` commands show 88 dynamically-discovered chat models, image providers configured, audio providers configured: Onboard summary — default model auto-selected from discovery: Model picker — full discovered DeepInfra catalog (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Google, Meta, MoonshotAI, NVIDIA, Qwen, ZAI, ...): Webchat session JSONL — image-gen → VLM → STT → TTS, all positive outcomes, redacted: [f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172089/f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log) Generated image (1792×1024 JPEG produced by the `image_generate` tool): Passive STT — agent responds to the auto-transcribed inbound audio attachment: STT input: [bobbyanaya-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172146/bobbyanaya-texas-prison-slang-three-greetings-same-meaning-whatx27s-up-165808.mp3) TTS — `tts` tool fires; the chat UI renders a playable audio bubble: TTS output: [voice-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172138/voice-1779514789533---942686ed-8f59-4165-95ce-8e588eafff99.mp3) Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` marker captured on the outgoing HTTPS body to DeepInfra via a logging proxy — proves the deepinfra-local cache wrapper fires where the upstream OpenRouter wrapper would have short-circuited: **Observed result after fix:** Six surfaces work end-to-end on live deployment: dynamic chat catalog (88 models), live image-gen catalog (FLUX), live audio provider catalog, in-chat image generation produces an on-disk JPEG, in-chat passive STT transcribes inbound audio, in-chat TTS produces a playable MP3 attachment, and Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers reach DeepInfra's API on `anthropic/*` requests. `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 69/69 green; `pnpm check:changed` lanes → exit 0. **What was not tested:** Long-form video-gen completion (the request fires successfully; the upstream model just doesn't finish within the `fetchWithSsrFGuard` 60 s window — separate upstream concern); the cache-marker test request itself returned an empty response from Anthropic (unrelated upstream model behavior), but the marker injection is independently captured at the wire by the proxy and shown above. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - **Default chat model**: users on the *implicit* DeepInfra default now route to `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash` (Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B has been retired from the active catalog). Anyone who pinned a model explicitly via config is unaffected. - Backward compatible at the config layer; no schema changes, no env var renames, no migration script. ### Auth-context threading fix `provider-discovery.ts` now passes the resolved catalog context (`env`, `agentDir`, and `hasApiKey=true` once `buildSingleProviderApiKeyCatalog` has verified the key resolves) through to `discoverDeepInfraModels`. Without this, an auth-profile-only setup could authenticate at the discovery layer yet still receive the static catalog because `hasDeepInfraApiKey` had no `agentDir` to probe the profile store. Both env-var and auth-profile-only setups now receive the live 88-model catalog identically. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed.

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded,
misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split,
each burning 3 first-attempt + 3 oversize-fallback retries per call. With N splits,
this burns N × 6 × chunkTokens before finally returning an unusable result.

This adds a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:
- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

## What changed

- Added `FallbackSummaryResult` type: `{ text: string; isGenericFallback: boolean }`
- Updated `summarizeWithFallback` to return `FallbackSummaryResult` with typed fallback signal
- Circuit breaker opens at 2 consecutive fallback splits, breaking the loop
- Counter resets to 0 on successful split (intermittent failures are fine)
- 3 new tests in `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts` with direct call-count assertions

## Review iteration

**v1 → v2**: moved the circuit breaker from `summarizeChunks` to `summarizeInStages`.
`summarizeChunks` has all catch paths throwing, so the loop never reaches a second
chunk after the first failure — a counter inside that loop can never reach the
threshold. The actual cross-split token burn happens at the `summarizeInStages`
split-loop level where each `summarizeWithFallback` call independently burns
retries.

**v2 → v3**: replaced text-prefix detection (`startsWith("Context contained")`) with
typed internal signal (`FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`) per ClawSweeper P2 review.
This avoids false positives when a successful summary begins with the fallback prefix.
Also added direct call-count assertion proving remaining splits and merge are skipped.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: When the compaction summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3 retry attempts. With N splits, this burns N × 3 × chunkTokens before finally returning an unusable result.

**Real environment tested**: Linux Node 22.22.0, Vitest test suite with mocked `generateSummary` simulating persistent summarizer unavailability.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/compaction-circuit-breaker-proof.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
✓ |agents| src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts (3 tests) 109ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
```

**Observed result after fix**:
1. Circuit breaker triggers after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Call count assertion `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(6)` proves:
   - Split 1: 3 calls (fail, consecutive: 1)
   - Split 2: 3 calls (fail, consecutive: 2 → breaker opens)
   - Splits 3-5: SKIPPED (0 calls)
   - Merge retry: SKIPPED (0 calls)
   - Total: 6 calls instead of 15+ (saves ~60% token burn)
3. Normal path completes all splits + merge when no consecutive failures

**What was not tested**: Live compaction with real summarizer model switching between available/unavailable (requires production environment).

## Verification

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts
```

Fixes #58838

target.title: fix(acp): treat missing cwd as stale bound session
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: bound ACP sessions stayed attached after the runtime `cwd` disappeared, so retries kept failing against a dead session.
- Why it matters: affected threads remain wedged until someone manually clears the stale binding.
- What changed: treat missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors as stale-session signals in both dispatch-time cleanup and reset-in-place cleanup, and cover both paths with regressions.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stale cleanup still only triggers for `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`; this does not broaden cleanup to generic turn failures.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #60720
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: stale-session detection only recognized metadata and resource-not-found init failures, so a missing ACP runtime working directory did not trigger stale unbind.
- Missing detection / guardrail: reset-in-place failure handling returned the error without clearing the stale binding, and no regression covered missing-`cwd` init failures.
- Contributing context (if known): the old fix branch had drifted too far from current `main`, but the bug still reproduced on April 18, 2026.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`, `src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors unbind stale bindings during both dispatch and reset-in-place recovery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: both failure paths are local runtime/control-plane seams and do not need a live ACP backend to prove the cleanup behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Bound ACP conversations recover automatically when the runtime working directory is gone instead of staying wedged behind a stale binding.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[bound ACP turn/reset] -> [missing cwd init failure] -> [binding remains] -> [next attempt fails again]

After:
[bound ACP turn/reset] -> [missing cwd init failure] -> [stale binding removed] -> [next attempt can recreate]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: NixOS Linux
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22.21.1, pnpm 11.1.0, OpenClaw package 2026.5.12-beta.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP persistent bindings
- Relevant config (redacted): session-bound ACP conversation with runtime `cwd` configured

### Steps

1. Reproduce an `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` error whose message is `ACP runtime working directory does not exist: ...` on a bound ACP session.
2. Trigger either dispatch-time reply handling or `resetAcpSessionInPlace` for that session.
3. Observe whether the session binding is removed.

### Expected

- The stale binding is removed and the failure is returned as skipped/recoverable cleanup, so the next attempt can recreate state.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale binding remained attached and the same failure repeated.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reproduced the missing-`cwd` classification gap against current `main`; ran targeted ACP dispatch/reset tests after the fix.
- Edge cases checked: stale-binding cleanup failure still returns the original skipped error instead of masking it.
- What you did **not** verify: live ACP backend behavior outside the focused test harness.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: stale-session cleanup could be triggered too broadly.
  - Mitigation: the shared helper still requires `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, and the new coverage locks the intended missing-`cwd` path specifically.

## AI Assistance

- AI was used to reproduce the current bug on `main`, implement the fix, run targeted tests, and draft this PR body.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A bound ACP session whose persisted runtime `cwd` no longer exists should be treated as stale, unbind the affected conversation, and return recoverable/skipped cleanup instead of keeping the stale binding attached.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on NixOS Linux; Node.js v22.21.1; pnpm 11.1.0; OpenClaw package version 2026.5.12-beta.1; base `origin/main` at `9eed27a9b8`; branch `fix/acp-stale-cwd-replacement` at `66007fdd20`. This used the actual OpenClaw repo test runner and source tree.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts -t resetAcpSessionInPlace
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
timeout -k5s 300s codex review --base origin/main
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  41 passed (41)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.42s

$ pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts -t resetAcpSessionInPlace
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed | 4 skipped (11)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 4.10s

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 25ms on 5 files using 1 threads.

$ timeout -k5s 300s codex review --base origin/main
The change consistently extends stale ACP session detection to missing working directories and unbinds affected session bindings in both dispatch and configured-binding reset paths. I did not find a discrete correctness issue introduced by the diff.
```

- **What was not tested**: Full live ACP backend recovery outside this local OpenClaw checkout was not exercised; the proof is focused on the implicated OpenClaw dispatch/reset seams.

target.title: fix(gateway): restore all-agent session discovery in sessions.usage when no agentId is provided
target.body:
## Summary

- Backend: when no `agentId` is provided in `sessions.usage` requests, discover sessions across all configured agents instead of defaulting to "main"
- Frontend: add `usageAgentId` state and agent selector dropdown to the usage page so operators can optionally scope usage queries to a specific agent
- Fixes the Web UI usage page agent filter that was broken since v2026.5.17 (commit 6a12c6f79915 / PR #81386)

## Behavior addressed

The v2026.5.17 fix scoped `sessions.usage` lookups by `agentId`, defaulting to "main" when no `agentId` was specified. The Web UI frontend never sent `agentId` in API requests, so non-main agent sessions were never returned, and the client-side agent filter popover only saw main sessions.

target.title: fix(hooks): enforce default hook agent allowlist
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens Gateway hook agent routing for https://github.com/NVIDIA-dev/openclaw-tracking/issues/685 and GHSA-724r-v4wf-mqc5.

When `hooks.allowedAgentIds` is configured, omitted or blank `agentId` now checks the effective default agent before dispatch instead of bypassing the allowlist.

## Changes

- Enforce `hooks.allowedAgentIds` against the effective default agent when hook callers omit or blank `agentId`.
- Add helper and Gateway server regressions for omitted, blank, default-allowed, wildcard, empty allowlist, explicit, unknown fallback, and mapped hook routing cases.
- Align config type/help text, security audit remediation copy, docs, and the generated config baseline hash with the effective-agent semantics.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check src/gateway/hooks.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/config/types.hooks.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/security/audit-extra.sync.ts`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/automation/cron-jobs.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
- `pnpm lint:docs docs/automation/cron-jobs.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean, no accepted/actionable findings)

## Notes

AI-assisted: yes, Codex.

Real behavior proof:

Behavior addressed: Hook agent allowlists now apply to the effective target agent, including default-agent routing when `agentId` is omitted or blank.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `1a1496e945a3` with real `openclaw gateway run` processes on loopback, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and live `curl` requests to `/hooks/agent`. Config A used agents `main` (default) and `hooks` with `hooks.allowedAgentIds: ["hooks"]`; Config B used the same agents with `hooks.allowedAgentIds: ["main"]`. The hook bearer token was redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the restrictive Gateway with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<tmp>/restrictive.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state-restrictive OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 corepack pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port 18798 --force`, sent the three redacted curl requests shown below, stopped that Gateway, then started the default-allowed Gateway with the same command shape on port `18799` and sent the omitted-`agentId` curl request shown below.

Evidence after fix:

```text
gateway restrictive ready: port=18798 sha=1a1496e945a3
case: restrictive allowlist, omitted agentId
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof omitted","deliver":false}
status: 400
response: {"ok":false,"error":"agentId is not allowed by hooks.allowedAgentIds"}
case: restrictive allowlist, blank agentId
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof blank","agentId":"   ","deliver":false}
status: 400
response: {"ok":false,"error":"agentId is not allowed by hooks.allowedAgentIds"}
case: restrictive allowlist, explicit allowed hooks agent
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof allowed","agentId":"hooks","sessionKey":"hook:explicit","deliver":false}
status: 200
response: {"ok":true,"runId":"7f86bba0-e7b5-447b-a6d7-2fb011cba50d"}
gateway default-allowed ready: port=18799 sha=1a1496e945a3
case: default-agent allowlist, omitted agentId
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof default allowed","deliver":false}
status: 200
response: {"ok":true,"runId":"538f275f-f661-4b1b-918f-23d6ed9712d4"}
```

Observed result after fix: Omitted and blank `agentId` are denied with HTTP 400 when the effective default agent is not allowlisted; an explicit allowlisted `hooks` agent route is accepted with HTTP 200 and a real `runId`; omitted `agentId` is accepted with HTTP 200 and a real `runId` when the default agent `main` is allowlisted.

What was not tested: No public webhook deployment, full repository Vitest suite, or real model completion was run. The accepted hook routes used `deliver:false`; the proof targets the Gateway hook routing/allowlist behavior ClawSweeper requested.
