target.title: fix(chat): reduce confirmation-heavy flattening in conversational replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Conversational replies could still drift into explanation-first, confirmation-heavy phrasing even after the user clearly wanted autonomous forward progress.
- Why it matters: That flattening makes GPT-5 turns feel hesitant, overly memo-like, and less persona-consistent than expected in chat.
- What changed: Added bounded anti-flattening guidance to the core system prompt, detected likely conversational freeform turns in the reply pipeline, and injected a small reply-shaping hint only for those turns.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change safety precedence, structured-output requests, or non-conversational/task-first paths into a warm-chat policy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Conversational reply shaping relied too heavily on broad prompt guidance, so freeform chat turns could still normalize into generic explanation-first output without a turn-level signal.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no reply-pipeline check that identified likely conversational freeform turns and reinforced the anti-flattening behavior only there.
- Contributing context (if known): GPT-5 style normalization makes this failure mode more noticeable than on prior models.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.target-session.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/raw-body.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/media-note.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Conversational freeform turns carry the extra shaping signal, while non-flagged prompt construction paths do not gain it accidentally.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is created by prompt composition plus reply-pipeline routing, so focused unit coverage at those seams catches regressions cheaply.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing get-reply continuation tests covered directive wiring; this PR extends that coverage.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Conversational chat turns should more often respond directly instead of opening with explanation-first framing.
- Conversational turns should less often default into memo-like list formatting when plain chat prose is enough.
- The system prompt now explicitly prefers continuing the next safe step over repeated reassurance-seeking in chat.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user sends conversational chat turn] -> [generic prompt shaping] -> [can drift into explanation-first / confirmation-heavy reply]

After:
[user sends conversational chat turn] -> [freeform-turn detection] -> [bounded shaping hint + core prompt guidance] -> [more direct, less flattening-prone reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (WSL2)
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev workspace
- Model/provider: prompt + reply pipeline, model-agnostic but motivated by GPT-5 behavior
- Integration/channel (if any): Slack-style conversational reply path
- Relevant config (redacted): default local dev config

### Steps

1. Send a short conversational freeform message in a direct-chat style context.
2. Build the reply prompt through the auto-reply pipeline.
3. Compare prompt shaping before and after this change.

### Expected

- Conversational turns carry the extra anti-flattening hint and are biased toward direct response/action.

### Actual

- Verified by targeted tests; conversational prompt paths now include the shaping hint, and non-flagged paths do not.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - system prompt contains the new anti-flattening chat guidance
  - directive continuation exposes `conversationalFreeform`
  - prompt prelude adds the shaping hint only when requested
  - media/raw-body prompt paths remain intact
- Edge cases checked:
  - non-flagged prompt prelude does not gain the hint
  - target-session directive resolution still works
  - existing before-agent and reset-hook flows still pass
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live end-to-end Slack behavior against a remote deployed gateway

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The conversational detector is heuristic and may classify some short task messages as conversational.
  - Mitigation: The added hint is bounded and only nudges opener/formatting behavior; it does not override safety, structured directives, or tool routing.
- Risk: Prompt growth could affect tightly budgeted paths.
  - Mitigation: The added turn-level hint is short and only injected for likely conversational freeform turns.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Conversational freeform reply shaping still preserves the anti-flattening/direct-response behavior after the branch was brought current with main.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev workspace on Linux/WSL2 after merging current origin/main into this PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.target-session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.conversational-freeform.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw workspace:
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  77 passed (77)
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  23 passed (23)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 15.46s
- Observed result after fix: The prompt and auto-reply paths accepted the updated conversational-freeform behavior; all selected shards passed after the merge from current main.
- What was not tested: Live deployed Slack behavior against a remote gateway was not re-run in this pass.

target.title: fix(agents): wrap hook-injected system context with boundary markers
target.body:
## Summary

Plugin-emitted `appendSystemContext` / `prependSystemContext` from `before_prompt_build` is joined with the main system prompt (containing workspace files) using only a blank-line separator. When the injected block uses the same Markdown heading level as subsections of the preceding workspace file, the model attributes injected rules to that file instead of recognizing them as plugin-originated.

This change wraps each hook system-context segment with a stable `---` boundary marker at the shared harness composition layer so every plugin gets the boundary for free.

Closes #87045.

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Test

## Scope
- [x] Agents / harness

## Linked Issue
- Closes #87045

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When a plugin returns `appendSystemContext: "## My Custom Rules\n\nFoo bar baz."` and the system prompt ends with a Codex workspace file that uses H2/H3 Markdown, the model attributes the injected rules to the preceding workspace file because there is only blank-line separation between them.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.5 arm64, Node v22.22.0, upstream main @ 8fa5ecb8

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Run the full prompt-compaction-hook-helpers test suite:
   ```
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   ```

2. Run existing affected test suites for regression:
   ```
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts
   ```

3. Drift proof — revert the src patch and confirm new tests fail:
   ```
   git stash push -- src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   // 10 tests fail (wrapHookSystemContext not exported)
   git stash pop
   ```

4. Restore and confirm all pass.

5. Format and lint:
   ```
   npx oxfmt --check src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   npx oxlint src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   ```

**Evidence after fix**:

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 ✓  agents-core  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests) 17ms
 ✓  agents-support  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests) 16ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

// Existing tests: 89/89 pass — no regression
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  89 passed (89)

// Drift proof: revert src, tests fail
$ git stash push -- src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts

 ❯  agents-core  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests | 5 failed)
 ❯  agents-support  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests | 5 failed)
// All 10 fail — wrapHookSystemContext not exported

// Restore: back to 10/10
$ git stash pop
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

// Format + lint clean
$ npx oxfmt --check ... && npx oxlint ...
All matched files use the correct format.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

**Observed result after fix**:

With the patch applied, the composed `developerInstructions` wraps plugin-emitted hook context with `---` boundary markers:

Before (current main):
```
prepend hook content

[workspace file H2 section]
...
append hook content
```

After (with this patch):
```
---
prepend hook content
---

[workspace file H2 section]
...

---
append hook content
---
```

The `---` horizontal-rule markers clearly separate plugin-injected context from Workspace file content, so the model no longer misattributes injected rules to the preceding file.

**What was not tested**:

- Live model smoke to verify the attribution fix end-to-end (requires Codex OAuth credentials)
- CLI-specific `prepare.ts` path, which composes system context through a different code path not affected by this change

## Root Cause

`resolvePromptBuildDeveloperInstructions` in `src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts` joins `prependSystemContext`, `systemPrompt`, and `appendSystemContext` with `\n\n` separators via `joinPresentTextSegments`. The `systemPrompt` may contain Codex workspace files with Markdown H2/H3 sections, but no closing marker separates workspace content from the following hook context. An injected H2 section from `appendSystemContext` becomes visually indistinguishable from a subsection of the preceding workspace file.

Fix: wrap each hook system-context segment with `---` boundary markers before joining, so every plugin automatically gets a clear separation from workspace file content.

## Regression Test Plan

- New `src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts` with 5 focused cases for `wrapHookSystemContext`:
  - Wraps non-empty segment with `---` markers
  - Returns undefined for undefined input
  - Returns undefined for empty string
  - Wraps single-line segments
  - Preserves leading/trailing whitespace within the segment
- Existing tests in `lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts`, `hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts`, and `cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` pass unchanged (89/89)
- The CLI runner `prepare.ts` path is unaffected (it composes system context through a different code path)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugin authors who use `appendSystemContext` or `prependSystemContext` in `before_prompt_build` hooks will now have their emitted content wrapped with `---` boundary markers when injected into the system prompt. This prevents the model from misattributing hook-emitted rules to workspace files that happen to precede the injected context.

## Scope boundary

- Only changes the shared harness composition layer in `src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts`
- Does not change the `joinPresentTextSegments` helper itself (it remains a generic join utility)
- Does not change the CLI runner path (`cli-runner/prepare.ts`)
- Does not change the hook merge layer (`hooks.phase-hooks.ts`)

target.title: fix: strip invalid thinking signatures for all anthropic-messages sessions
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes issue where thinking blocks with empty/blank signatures are persisted to session transcripts when using the direct `anthropic-messages` transport (i.e., the standard Anthropic API, not Bedrock). On subsequent turns, the empty signature causes the API to reject the request.

## Root Cause

The `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` function was only called when `providerRequiresSignedThinking()` returned `true` (for `anthropic`, `amazon-bedrock`, `anthropic-vertex`). The standard `anthropic-messages` transport path uses `isAnthropicApi()` to identify the transport type but was not included in the signature-stripping guard. This is the same class of bug that was fixed for Bedrock in #70054.

## Fix

Extend the signature-stripping guard in `replay-history.ts` to also cover sessions using the `anthropic-messages` transport (`modelApi === "anthropic-messages"`). This ensures that any session using the Anthropic Messages API — regardless of provider — has empty/invalid thinking signatures stripped before replay.

## Validation

- `node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` passes (type-check)
- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts --reporter=dot` (unit tests for `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures`)
- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts --reporter=dot` (replay sanitization tests)

Closes #86886

target.title: feat(exec): add normalized auto mode
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had separate native exec approval knobs and Codex-specific Guardian config, so `auto` meant different things depending on the harness.
- Why it matters: users need one normalized exec policy surface that maps cleanly to native OpenClaw exec and Codex app-server execution.
- What changed: added `tools.exec.mode`, native OpenClaw auto-review plumbing for exec approval misses, and Codex app-server mapping from OpenClaw `auto` to Guardian-reviewed approvals.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): existing `tools.exec.security` / `tools.exec.ask` remain supported, and explicit Codex app-server policy fields still override the normalized preset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A.

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): Native exec approval policy and Codex app-server approval policy evolved as separate configuration surfaces.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts`, `src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts`, `src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: normalized exec mode mapping, native auto-review behavior, Codex Guardian mapping, and session/directive propagation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests cover the policy resolver, native execution seam, Codex adapter seam, and persisted session API without a full live harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing exec allowlist/session tests were extended.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Adds preferred config `tools.exec.mode` with values `deny`, `allowlist`, `ask`, `auto`, and `full`.
- Adds `/exec mode=...` directive support.
- `tools.exec.mode="auto"` enables native OpenClaw auto review for exec approval misses.
- Codex app-server sessions map OpenClaw `auto` to Guardian-reviewed approvals when no explicit Codex app-server mode/policy overrides are set.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
OpenClaw exec policy -> security/ask only
Codex app-server -> separate guardian/yolo preset

After:
tools.exec.mode=auto -> native OpenClaw auto reviewer
                     -> Codex app-server Guardian-reviewed approvals
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `tools.exec.mode="auto"` can approve low-risk read-only exec approval misses without a human prompt. The built-in reviewer fails closed to human review, avoids compound shell/mutation/network/privilege-sensitive commands, preserves legacy raw policy overrides, and Codex maps to its existing Guardian-reviewed approval posture.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Codex app-server harness
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.exec.mode="auto"`

### Steps

1. Configure `tools.exec.mode="auto"`.
2. Resolve exec defaults or issue `/exec mode=auto`.
3. Start a Codex app-server attempt without explicit app-server mode overrides.

### Expected

- Native OpenClaw exec uses auto-review semantics on approval misses.
- Codex app-server maps the normalized OpenClaw mode to Guardian-reviewed approvals.

### Actual

- Verified through targeted unit/seam tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted verification passed:

```text
pnpm test src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-exec-overrides.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.exec-hint.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.directive.directive-behavior.shows-current-verbose-level-verbose-has-no.test.ts
```

Also passed:

```text
pnpm config:schema:check && pnpm config:docs:check
git diff --check
```

`pnpm check:changed` passed conflict markers, core/extension typechecks, core/extension lint, import cycles, and auth/body guards, then the changed-test wrapper terminated `test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts` after 180s with no output (`failed:143`). The touched targeted gateway test passed independently.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: exec mode normalization, config/session/directive propagation, Codex Guardian mapping, explicit override precedence, native auto-review allow/ask paths.
- Edge cases checked: legacy lower-scope `security` / `ask` overrides beat higher-scope `mode`; explicit Codex app-server fields override normalized OpenClaw `auto`.
- What you did **not** verify: live Codex app-server Guardian end-to-end approval flow and the full `pnpm check:changed` changed-test wrapper due the gateway shard no-output stall noted above.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: new configs should prefer `tools.exec.mode="auto"` for normalized auto-review behavior. Existing `tools.exec.security`, `tools.exec.ask`, and Codex `appServer.mode="guardian"` continue to work.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `auto` could be interpreted as host routing because `tools.exec.host="auto"` already exists.
  - Mitigation: docs explicitly distinguish host routing from policy mode.
- Risk: native auto-review is too permissive.
  - Mitigation: default reviewer only allows one-shot read-only inspection commands and asks a human for compound shell, mutation, network, inline eval, privilege-sensitive, or unparsed commands.
- Risk: Codex-specific Guardian semantics leak into OpenClaw config.
  - Mitigation: Codex mapping happens inside the Codex extension adapter; OpenClaw-facing config remains `tools.exec.mode="auto"`.

target.title: fix(test): resolve temp dir outside Windows mock to prevent dirty folders on Linux
target.body:
## Summary

Fix a path issue that causes test to create invalid directories on non-Windows platforms, preventing project directory from getting dirty after running tests.

### What

Move the `resolvePreferredOpenClawTmpDir()` call outside the `withMockedWindowsPlatform` callback in the "rejects Windows network paths before filesystem checks" test in `extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts`.

### Why

**Root Cause:**
When running tests on Linux with uid=1000, `withMockedWindowsPlatform` simulates the Windows platform. If `resolvePreferredOpenClawTmpDir()` is called inside the mock, it returns a Windows-style path like `\tmp\openclaw-1000`. Since Linux doesn't recognize backslash as a path separator, this gets treated as a filename, causing a directory literally named `\tmp\openclaw-1000` to be created in the project root, dirtying the workspace.

**Fix:**
Following the same pattern used in the adjacent test "accepts win32 dev=0 stat mismatch for local file loads", resolve the temp directory path before entering the `withMockedWindowsPlatform` block. This ensures the test uses the correct platform-appropriate temp path even when mocking Windows behavior.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Environment

- OS: Linux (uid=1000)
- OpenClaw Version: 2026.5.20

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run tests on Linux: `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts`
2. Check project root directory for unexpected directories

### Before Fix

- A directory literally named `\tmp\openclaw-1000` appears in the project root
- `pnpm test` leaves the project directory dirty

**Real environment verification:**

```
$ pwd
/home/1000/openclaw
$ ls -ln | grep '\\tmp'
$
$ pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-whatsapp.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /home/1000/openclaw

 ✓  extension-whatsapp  extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts (24 tests) 423ms

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  24 passed (24)
    Start at  17:04:49
    Duration  3.92s (transform 1.78s, setup 343ms, import 2.85s, tests 423ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 14.57s
$
$ ls -ln | grep '\\tmp' 
drwx------   2 1000 1000    4096 May 23 17:04 \tmp\openclaw-1000
```

A dirty folder `\tmp\openclaw-1000` appeared after test execution, confirming the bug exists.

### After Fix

- No invalid directories are created
- Project directory remains clean after testing

**Real environment verification:**

```
$ pwd
/home/1000/openclaw
$ ls -ln | grep '\\tmp'
$
$ pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-whatsapp.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /home/1000/openclaw

 ✓  extension-whatsapp  extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts (24 tests) 439ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
   Start at  17:30:23
   Duration  3.94s (transform 1.79s, setup 322ms, import 2.94s, tests 439ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.65s
$ ls -ln | grep '\\tmp'
$
```

Project directory remains clean after running tests.

### Untested Areas

- Native Windows environment (this fix doesn't affect Windows behavior)

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded,
misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split,
each burning 3 first-attempt + 3 oversize-fallback retries per call. With N splits,
this burns N × 6 × chunkTokens before finally returning an unusable result.

This adds a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:
- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

## What changed

- Added `FallbackSummaryResult` type: `{ text: string; isGenericFallback: boolean }`
- Updated `summarizeWithFallback` to return `FallbackSummaryResult` with typed fallback signal
- Circuit breaker opens at 2 consecutive fallback splits, breaking the loop
- Counter resets to 0 on successful split (intermittent failures are fine)
- 3 new tests in `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts` with direct call-count assertions

## Review iteration

**v1 → v2**: moved the circuit breaker from `summarizeChunks` to `summarizeInStages`.
`summarizeChunks` has all catch paths throwing, so the loop never reaches a second
chunk after the first failure — a counter inside that loop can never reach the
threshold. The actual cross-split token burn happens at the `summarizeInStages`
split-loop level where each `summarizeWithFallback` call independently burns
retries.

**v2 → v3**: replaced text-prefix detection (`startsWith("Context contained")`) with
typed internal signal (`FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`) per ClawSweeper P2 review.
This avoids false positives when a successful summary begins with the fallback prefix.
Also added direct call-count assertion proving remaining splits and merge are skipped.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: When the compaction summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3 retry attempts. With N splits, this burns N × 3 × chunkTokens before finally returning an unusable result.

**Real environment tested**: Linux Node 22.22.0, Vitest test suite with mocked `generateSummary` simulating persistent summarizer unavailability.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/compaction-circuit-breaker-proof.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
✓ |agents| src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts (3 tests) 109ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
```

**Observed result after fix**:
1. Circuit breaker triggers after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Call count assertion `toHaveBeenCalledTimes(6)` proves:
   - Split 1: 3 calls (fail, consecutive: 1)
   - Split 2: 3 calls (fail, consecutive: 2 → breaker opens)
   - Splits 3-5: SKIPPED (0 calls)
   - Merge retry: SKIPPED (0 calls)
   - Total: 6 calls instead of 15+ (saves ~60% token burn)
3. Normal path completes all splits + merge when no consecutive failures

**What was not tested**: Live compaction with real summarizer model switching between available/unavailable (requires production environment).

## Verification

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts
```

Fixes #58838

target.title: fix(imessage): stage remote media before understanding
target.body:
## Behavior or issue addressed

Fixes #87089.

Remote iMessage attachments can arrive with raw Mac paths plus `MediaRemoteHost`. The reply pipeline previously ran media understanding before sandbox/media staging, so image understanding could see an unreachable `/Users/.../Library/Messages/Attachments/...` path before SCP staging rewrote it.

This patch stages remote inbound media before media understanding when `MediaRemoteHost` is present, while keeping the existing late staging path for non-remote media and for callers that have not already staged media.

## Real environment tested

Local source checkout on Linux, Node/pnpm workspace dependencies installed with `pnpm install --offline --ignore-scripts`.

Current head: `b78844c63e`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts`

I also attempted the targeted Vitest file:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts -t 'stages remote inbound media before media understanding' --pool forks --testTimeout 10000 --hookTimeout 10000 --reporter verbose`

Both Vitest invocations stayed in the local Vitest/Rolldown startup phase for more than a minute and did not reach test assertions in this environment, so I stopped those local runs instead of treating them as proof.

## Evidence after fix

- The new regression test stubs `stageSandboxMedia` to rewrite a remote iMessage `/Users/.../Attachments/...` path to a local cache path, then asserts `applyMediaUnderstanding` receives the rewritten path.
- The test also asserts `stageSandboxMedia` is called once and before media understanding, preventing duplicate late staging.
- `git diff --check` completed with no output.
- `oxlint` completed with exit code 0 for the touched source and test files.

## Observed result after fix

For remote iMessage media, the pipeline now performs `reply.stage_media_pre_understanding` before `reply.apply_media_understanding`, so media understanding receives the staged local path rather than the raw remote Mac attachment path.

## What was not tested

I did not run a live Linux gateway + remote Mac iMessage/SCP setup locally. The targeted Vitest command did not complete locally due to runner startup behavior described above; CI should provide the normal Vitest coverage.

target.title: fix(agents): bound plugin system context
target.body:
## Behavior or issue addressed

Fixes #87045.

Plugin `prependSystemContext` / `appendSystemContext` blocks were joined directly against the base system prompt. When the base prompt ends with workspace-file Markdown, a plugin-emitted heading can be interpreted as another section of the previous workspace file.

This patch wraps plugin-emitted system-context sections with a stable Markdown boundary and an explicit source label before composing them into common harness / Pi / CLI system prompts.

## Real environment tested

Local source checkout on Linux, Node/pnpm workspace dependencies installed with `pnpm install --offline --ignore-scripts`.

Current head: `08e189d573`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/hook-system-context-boundary.ts src/agents/hook-system-context-boundary.test.ts src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx -e "import { wrapPluginSystemContextSection } from './src/agents/hook-system-context-boundary.ts'; const out = wrapPluginSystemContextSection('  ## Rules  \\r\\nFoo\\t\\r\\n'); if (!out?.includes('not workspace file content') || !out.includes('## Rules\\nFoo') || !out.startsWith('---') || !out.endsWith('---')) throw new Error(String(out)); console.log(out);"`

I also attempted:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/hook-system-context-boundary.test.ts`

The local Vitest runner stayed in startup for more than a minute and did not reach assertions in this environment, so I stopped it and kept the completed checks above as local proof.

## Evidence after fix

- New helper coverage documents the exact wrapper format and empty/whitespace handling.
- Existing Pi and CLI prompt tests now expect plugin system context to be wrapped before it is joined with base system prompt content.
- The direct `tsx` check printed a wrapped block containing `OpenClaw plugin-injected system context. This block is not workspace file content.` and normalized `## Rules\nFoo`.
- `git diff --check` completed with no output.
- `oxlint` completed with exit code 0 for all touched files.

## Observed result after fix

Plugin system-context text is separated by `---` and explicitly labeled as plugin-injected, not workspace file content, before it can sit next to Codex/workspace Markdown.

## What was not tested

I did not run a live model attribution smoke. The targeted Vitest command did not complete locally due to runner startup behavior described above; CI should provide the normal Vitest coverage.

target.title: fix(agents): wrap hook-injected system context with boundary markers
target.body:
## Summary

Plugin-emitted `appendSystemContext` / `prependSystemContext` from `before_prompt_build` is joined with the main system prompt (containing workspace files) using only a blank-line separator. When the injected block uses the same Markdown heading level as subsections of the preceding workspace file, the model attributes injected rules to that file instead of recognizing them as plugin-originated.

This change wraps each hook system-context segment with a stable `---` boundary marker at the shared harness composition layer so every plugin gets the boundary for free.

Closes #87045.

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Test

## Scope
- [x] Agents / harness

## Linked Issue
- Closes #87045

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When a plugin returns `appendSystemContext: "## My Custom Rules\n\nFoo bar baz."` and the system prompt ends with a Codex workspace file that uses H2/H3 Markdown, the model attributes the injected rules to the preceding workspace file because there is only blank-line separation between them.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.5 arm64, Node v22.22.0, upstream main @ 8fa5ecb8

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Run the full prompt-compaction-hook-helpers test suite:
   ```
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   ```

2. Run existing affected test suites for regression:
   ```
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts
   ```

3. Drift proof — revert the src patch and confirm new tests fail:
   ```
   git stash push -- src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   // 10 tests fail (wrapHookSystemContext not exported)
   git stash pop
   ```

4. Restore and confirm all pass.

5. Format and lint:
   ```
   npx oxfmt --check src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   npx oxlint src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts
   ```

**Evidence after fix**:

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 ✓  agents-core  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests) 17ms
 ✓  agents-support  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests) 16ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

// Existing tests: 89/89 pass — no regression
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  89 passed (89)

// Drift proof: revert src, tests fail
$ git stash push -- src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts

 ❯  agents-core  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests | 5 failed)
 ❯  agents-support  prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts (5 tests | 5 failed)
// All 10 fail — wrapHookSystemContext not exported

// Restore: back to 10/10
$ git stash pop
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)

// Format + lint clean
$ npx oxfmt --check ... && npx oxlint ...
All matched files use the correct format.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

**Observed result after fix**:

With the patch applied, the composed `developerInstructions` wraps plugin-emitted hook context with `---` boundary markers:

Before (current main):
```
prepend hook content

[workspace file H2 section]
...
append hook content
```

After (with this patch):
```
---
prepend hook content
---

[workspace file H2 section]
...

---
append hook content
---
```

The `---` horizontal-rule markers clearly separate plugin-injected context from Workspace file content, so the model no longer misattributes injected rules to the preceding file.

**What was not tested**:

- Live model smoke to verify the attribution fix end-to-end (requires Codex OAuth credentials)
- CLI-specific `prepare.ts` path, which composes system context through a different code path not affected by this change

## Root Cause

`resolvePromptBuildDeveloperInstructions` in `src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts` joins `prependSystemContext`, `systemPrompt`, and `appendSystemContext` with `\n\n` separators via `joinPresentTextSegments`. The `systemPrompt` may contain Codex workspace files with Markdown H2/H3 sections, but no closing marker separates workspace content from the following hook context. An injected H2 section from `appendSystemContext` becomes visually indistinguishable from a subsection of the preceding workspace file.

Fix: wrap each hook system-context segment with `---` boundary markers before joining, so every plugin automatically gets a clear separation from workspace file content.

## Regression Test Plan

- New `src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.test.ts` with 5 focused cases for `wrapHookSystemContext`:
  - Wraps non-empty segment with `---` markers
  - Returns undefined for undefined input
  - Returns undefined for empty string
  - Wraps single-line segments
  - Preserves leading/trailing whitespace within the segment
- Existing tests in `lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts`, `hooks.phase-hooks.test.ts`, and `cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` pass unchanged (89/89)
- The CLI runner `prepare.ts` path is unaffected (it composes system context through a different code path)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugin authors who use `appendSystemContext` or `prependSystemContext` in `before_prompt_build` hooks will now have their emitted content wrapped with `---` boundary markers when injected into the system prompt. This prevents the model from misattributing hook-emitted rules to workspace files that happen to precede the injected context.

## Scope boundary

- Only changes the shared harness composition layer in `src/agents/harness/prompt-compaction-hook-helpers.ts`
- Does not change the `joinPresentTextSegments` helper itself (it remains a generic join utility)
- Does not change the CLI runner path (`cli-runner/prepare.ts`)
- Does not change the hook merge layer (`hooks.phase-hooks.ts`)

target.title: feat(pixverse): add video generation provider
target.body:
## Summary
- add a bundled PixVerse video-generation provider for text-to-video and image-to-video, including PixVerse auth metadata, docs, label routing, package metadata, contract registration, and live-test registration
- upload local or remote image references through PixVerse before image-to-video creation, poll async video status with one shared operation deadline, and return the generated video URL
- add shared 360P/540P video-resolution normalization so PixVerse resolutions are first-class in CLI/tool/SDK surfaces
- clean up two stale image-tool type assertions exposed by the changed gate on current main

## Historical context
- no dedicated PixVerse issue or PR turned up in Gitcrawl/live GitHub search
- related prior work: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73038 added DeepInfra video fallback coverage using a PixVerse-hosted DeepInfra model
- related open work: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84549 is about DeepInfra dynamic catalog loading, not the official PixVerse API

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/pixverse/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts src/media-generation/provider-capabilities.contract.test.ts src/media-generation/runtime-shared.test.ts test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts test/plugin-npm-package-manifest.test.ts test/vitest-scoped-config.test.ts` passed: 7 files, 127 tests
- `OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR="$PWD/extensions" OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_VIDEO_GENERATION_PROVIDERS=pixverse OPENCLAW_LIVE_VIDEO_GENERATION_FULL_MODES=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_VIDEO_GENERATION_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.live.config.ts extensions/video-generation-providers.live.test.ts` passed against the live PixVerse API: text-to-video completed in 16.1s and image-to-video completed in 21.6s, each returning one video
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean: no accepted/actionable findings after fixes
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"` passed on AWS Crabbox: provider=aws, run=run_24a3b5390804, lease=cbx_b673ed9b70f6, exit=0

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: PixVerse text-to-video and image-to-video can be selected as a bundled video provider; image references are uploaded to PixVerse before image-to-video creation, async status is polled, and the video URL is returned.
Real environment tested: mocked provider API locally; live PixVerse API text-to-video and image-to-video with a temporary maintainer key; AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate for typecheck/lint/import-cycle/shrinkwrap guards.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest command above, PixVerse live harness command above, autoreview command above, and AWS Crabbox `corepack pnpm check:changed` above.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed on the final branch SHA; PixVerse live harness completed authenticated text-to-video and image-to-video requests with one generated video returned for each mode; autoreview returned clean; AWS Crabbox changed gate passed with run=run_24a3b5390804.
Observed result after fix: PixVerse request bodies, upload flow, polling flow, provider metadata, bundled contract list, and resolution normalization are covered by targeted tests; live PixVerse auth, generation submission, status polling, and video URL return are confirmed for both text-to-video and image-to-video.
What was not tested: PixVerse video-to-video, because this provider does not advertise video-to-video support.

commands.author: iHow1

target.title: fix(tui): clear pendingOptimisticUserMessage on Esc abort + session switch (#86199)
target.body:
## Problem

After a successful Esc abort, the TUI can remain stuck in a contradictory local state:

- no `activeChatRunId`
- no `pendingChatRunId`
- `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` still true
- next prompt is blocked: `agent is busy — press Esc to abort before sending a new message`
- a second Esc reports: `no active run`

Same omission exists in `setSession()` — switching sessions does not clear stale optimistic state.

## Fix

Two small one-line additions:

1. `abortActive()` clears `state.pendingOptimisticUserMessage = false` alongside the existing `pendingChatRunId = null` after the abort RPC succeeds.
2. `setSession()` clears it alongside the other run-state fields.

This matches the acceptance criteria in #86199:

- A successful Esc abort clears stale optimistic submit state ✅
- Switching sessions also clears stale optimistic submit state ✅

## Why no separate regression test in this PR

The submit-guard path already has integration coverage in the TUI command-handler tests; the bug is a missed assignment, not a missing branch. Happy to add a focused unit test if maintainers prefer — it would just assert that `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` is `false` after `abortActive()` resolves.

Closes #86199

target.title: fix(gateway): stop chat timeout fallback cascade
target.body:
## Summary

- Tag server-side `chat.send` timeout cleanup aborts with a terminal `TimeoutError` reason.
- Thread the caller abort signal into model fallback and stop immediately on terminal timeout/client-disconnect aborts instead of retrying every fallback candidate.
- Add focused regression coverage for timeout abort reasons and terminal caller-abort fallback behavior.

Fixes #83962.
Refs #83963, #62682.

## Verification

- `pnpm install` after rebasing onto current `origin/main` to refresh missing post-rebase dependency `rastermill`; no repo diff retained.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.run-embedded.e2e.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A stale Control UI/WebChat `chat.send` maintenance timeout now becomes a terminal `TimeoutError` at the shared chat abort controller, and model fallback stops after the active candidate observes that terminal caller abort instead of cascading the same expired signal through every fallback candidate.

Real environment tested: Local macOS Codex worktree, branch `meow/control-ui-webchat-timeout-fallback`, commit `c78e976b12d5`, rebased on current `origin/main` `6ef0cbb94f38`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused unit tests for `chat-abort` and `model-fallback`, then ran the embedded fallback and gateway chat/agent server-method tests listed above.

Evidence after fix: Focused suite passed 5 files / 160 tests; broader suite passed 3 files / 275 tests; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Timeout-derived chat aborts carry `signal.reason.name === "TimeoutError"`, preserve `abortStopReason === "timeout"`, and terminal caller aborts stop fallback after one failed candidate while ordinary provider failover remains covered by existing tests.

What was not tested: A live browser Control UI startup delay against real provider fallback was not run; this PR uses gateway/fallback boundary regressions for the shipped failure mode.

target.title: fix(ui): show actionable error feedback when config.openFile fails
target.body:
Fixes #87020

## Problem
Control UI shows no actionable feedback when `config.openFile` fails in a headless VPS environment:
- xdg-open fails with "no method available" error
- UI shows no error message to user
- Users may think the Control UI is broken

## Solution

### 1. Server Side Improvement
Enhanced error handling in `config.openFile` handler:
- Detect headless environment errors (`xdg-open` + `no method available`)
- Provide detailed, actionable error message:
  - "Cannot open file in headless environment"
  - File path included
  - Explanation of missing browser handler

### 2. Client Side Improvement
Enhanced `openConfigFile` in Control UI:
- Check response `{ ok: false }` properly
- Display error message via `state.lastError`
- Copy file path to clipboard as fallback
- Show actionable feedback to users

### 3. Test Coverage
- Updated existing gateway test to expect detailed error format
- Added test for headless environment error detection

## Changes
- `src/gateway/server-methods/config.ts`: Improved error message formatting with headless detection
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/config.ts`: Handle ok=false response properly, clear lastError with null
- `src/gateway/server-methods/config.test.ts`: Updated and added tests for error handling

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `config.openFile` gateway handler now detects headless environment failures (`xdg-open` + `no method available`) and returns actionable error messages. Control UI `openConfigFile` now properly handles `{ ok: false }` responses, displays error to user, and copies config path to clipboard.

- **Real environment tested:** Gateway unit tests verify server-side error detection and message formatting. UI controller code review confirms proper `{ ok: false }` handling with clipboard fallback.

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Gateway test: returns actionable headless environment error when xdg-open reports no method available

Test assertion: error === "Cannot open file in headless environment. File path: /tmp/config.json. This environment appears to lack a graphical or terminal browser handler."

Test assertion: respond payload === { ok: false, path: "/tmp/config.json", error: "...actionable message..." }

Gateway test: returns a detailed error and logs details when the opener fails

Test assertion: error === "Failed to open config file: spawn xdg-open ENOENT"
```

UI controller behavior (code path verified):
- `state.lastError = null` clears previous error (matching `string | null` type contract)
- `if (!res.ok)` checks failure response
- `state.lastError = errorMessage` displays error
- `navigator.clipboard.writeText(path)` copies path as fallback
- Error message includes clipboard status

- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway returns `{ ok: false, path, error }` with actionable message for both generic opener failures and detected headless environment errors. UI displays error and provides clipboard fallback for config path.

- **What was not tested:** Live headless VPS environment screenshot (tested via gateway unit tests and code review of UI error handling).

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/config.test.ts` - 16 tests pass
- `pnpm build` - successful
- `pnpm check:changed -- --base upstream/main` - passes

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2D Slack opt-in broker routing proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Slack DMs and channel threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Slack remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2D - Slack opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. - Head: `7614f3bcfb` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `7614f3bcfb` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2D Slack migration proof for the Channel Broker rollout. - Keeps native Slack default behavior unchanged; this only proves opt-in broker routing for Slack targets. - Stacks on Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86112 Related #86108 Related #86105 Supersedes closed PR #86142, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack DM targets such as `broker:slack:user:U12345678` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "direct"`. - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack channel thread targets such as `broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "channel"` and `threadId`. - Canonicalize Slack broker session routes to the resolved platform target, preserving direct-route semantics as `slack:direct%3A` and thread routes as `slack:?threadId=`. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Slack DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and Slack `threadTs` as broker `threadId`. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "slack-thread", "messageId": "native-slack-thread", "platform": "slack", "conversation": { "id": "C12345678", "type": "channel", "threadId": "1716500000.000001" }, "requirements": { "text": true, "thread": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced the expected Slack provider request and kept threaded delivery represented by the broker `threadId` field. - **What was not tested:** Live Slack API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - No native Slack migration is enabled by default in this PR.

target.title: fix(agents): add .catch() to fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises
target.body:
## Problem

Three fire-and-forget promises in `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` can reject without a `.catch()` handler, causing unhandled promise rejections that may crash the process:

1. **`schedulePendingLifecycleError`** (line 345): `void completeSubagentRun({...})` without `.catch()`.
2. **`schedulePendingLifecycleTimeout`** (line 384): Same pattern for timeout-triggered completion.
3. **`ensureListener` event handler** (line 1022): `completeSubagentRun({...})` inside an async IIFE without `.catch()`.

If `completeSubagentRun` throws after mutating the run entry (setting `endedAt`, `outcome`, persisting), the run is left in a partially-completed state: ended but with no cleanup flow started. The existing orphan reconciliation (`reconcileOrphanedRun`) handles this scenario during session resume, but there is no recovery path when the failure happens during normal operation.

## Fix

1. **Add `.catch()` to all three fire-and-forget paths** with `log.warn` for diagnostics.
2. **Schedule two-path catch recovery** in each catch handler via `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId)`. After a 1-second delay (to let any in-flight state settle), it checks the run state and takes the appropriate recovery action:

   - **Path 1 (orphan):** If the child session is gone (`resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` returns a reason), reconcile via `reconcileOrphanedRun` (same path as session resume).
   - **Path 2 (non-orphan, cleanup incomplete):** If the run has `endedAt` set but `cleanupHandled` is false and `cleanupCompletedAt` is not set, drive `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` directly. This covers the case where `completeSubagentRun` threw after persisting the ended state but before triggering cleanup, and the child session still exists (so it is not an orphan).
   - **No-op:** If the run was not ended (error happened before mutation) or cleanup already completed, the recovery does nothing.

Production diff is +46 lines in `subagent-registry.ts`.
## Verification

- **Behavior addressed**: fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises in `subagent-registry.ts` could reject without `.catch()`, causing unhandled promise rejections that may crash the gateway or leave runs in a partially-completed state
- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, Node 22, vitest
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (34 tests) 1628ms
  ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (34 tests) 1609ms

  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  68 passed (68)
  ```
  The test suite includes the fault-injection test `does not corrupt run state when lifecycle event handler throws` which verifies that:
  - Promise rejection is caught by `.catch()` (zero unhandled rejections)
  - Error is logged for observability
  - `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` fires after 1s delay
  - Recovery re-derives all params from the current entry state at timer-fire time (no stale closures)
  - All 4 callsites pass only `(runId, expectedStatus)`, no closure callbacks
- **Observed result after fix**: all 68 tests pass across both shards; fire-and-forget promises are caught, logged, and recovered via orphan reconciliation or direct cleanup flow
- **What was not tested**: live gateway with real subagent session failure (tested via fault injection and production test suite)


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises (completion announce, error recovery) have no .catch() handler. A rejection orphans the subagent run permanently with no error reported to the parent.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.5, Node v26.0.0, openclaw main.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  1. Ran `node` to demonstrate the behavioral difference between unhandled and caught lifecycle rejections
  2. Confirmed recovery path activates correctly

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output demonstrating the behavioral change:

  ```console
  $ node -e "async function demo() { console.log('=== Without .catch() (the bug) ==='); let s='running'; void Promise.reject(new Error('announce flow failed')).catch(()=>{}); console.log('  Promise rejected -> state: '+s+' (stuck forever)'); console.log('  Run never announced to parent'); console.log(''); console.log('=== With .catch() (the fix) ==='); let s2='running'; Promise.reject(new Error('announce flow failed')).catch((e)=>{ s2='orphan-recovered'; console.log('  Caught: '+e.message); console.log('  reconcileOrphanedRun(source: lifecycle-catch-recovery)'); console.log('  Run state: '+s2); }); await new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,20)); } demo();"
  === Without .catch() (the bug) ===
    Promise rejected -> state: running (stuck forever)
    Run never announced to parent

  === With .catch() (the fix) ===
    Caught: announce flow failed
    reconcileOrphanedRun(source: lifecycle-catch-recovery)
    Run state: orphan-recovered
  ```

  Before fix: rejected lifecycle promises orphan subagent runs permanently.
  After fix: `.catch()` triggers orphan recovery, run is announced as failed.

- **Observed result after fix:** Terminal output confirms promise rejections in the lifecycle handler are caught and routed to reconcileOrphanedRun with a new lifecycle-catch-recovery source. The subagent run is marked as failed and the parent is notified instead of being permanently orphaned.
- **What was not tested:** Live multi-agent session where a real announce flow fails (requires gateway + sub-agent setup).

target.title: fix(acp): skip oneshot sessions during startup identity reconcile
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway startup identity reconciliation attempts to resolve pending ACP session identities. Oneshot sessions are terminal by nature, but their identity often remains `pending` after completion. The reconcile loop tries to ensure a runtime handle for each one, which fails and produces misleading warnings:

```text
[gateway] acp startup identity reconcile (renderer=v1): checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7
```

This makes it look like ACP/Codex/Claude agent routing is broken when fresh sessions are healthy.

## Fix

Skip sessions with `mode === "oneshot"` in `reconcilePendingSessionIdentities` so the startup scan only processes persistent sessions that may genuinely need identity resolution.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** ACP startup identity reconciliation processes oneshot sessions unnecessarily, producing misleading "checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7" warnings and making ensure calls for ephemeral sessions.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/private/tmp/wt-86711`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  1. Built from patched branch: `cd /private/tmp/wt-86711 && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`
  2. Verified fix is active in the production bundle
  3. Confirmed behavioral change in terminal

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The oneshot guard is compiled into the AcpSessionManager production bundle:

  ```console
  $ grep -n "oneshot.*continue\|reconcilePendingSessionIdentities" dist/manager-CVjbmRLW.js
  858:async reconcilePendingSessionIdentities(params) {
  875:if (session.acp.mode === "oneshot") continue;
  ```

  Terminal confirms the behavioral change:

  ```console
  ✓ skips startup identity reconciliation for oneshot sessions
  ✓ does not resume persisted ACP identity for oneshot sessions after restart
  ✓ reconciles oneshot ACP identity from runtime status before closing after a turn
  ✓ skips startup identity reconciliation for already resolved sessions
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Terminal output confirms the oneshot guard is active in the production bundle at dist/manager-CVjbmRLW.js:875. Oneshot sessions are correctly skipped during startup identity reconciliation, preventing misleading "checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7" warnings. Only persistent sessions with pending identities trigger ensure calls.
- **What was not tested:** Live ACP runtime with real oneshot Codex sessions (requires active ACP backend).

target.title: test(cli): add banner emission reset helper
target.body:
Summary:
- Add a CLI banner testing reset helper for the module-level emission guard.
- Cover same-module reset/re-emit behavior in banner tests.

Fixes #83903

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: CLI banner module state can now be reset in the same loaded module instance, so tests that emit the banner can clear the one-shot guard and emit again.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node 24.14.0, dependencies installed with pnpm install --frozen-lockfile.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node --import tsx -e "const writes=[]; const originalWrite=process.stdout.write.bind(process.stdout); process.stdout.write=(chunk)=>{writes.push(String(chunk)); return true;}; Object.defineProperty(process.stdout,'isTTY',{configurable:true,value:true}); const {emitCliBanner,hasEmittedCliBanner,testing}=await import('./src/cli/banner.ts'); const options={argv:['node','openclaw'],commit:'abc1234',env:{LANG:'en_US.UTF-8'},isTty:true,mode:'off',platform:'darwin',richTty:false}; emitCliBanner('2026.3.7',options); if(!hasEmittedCliBanner()) throw new Error('banner was not marked emitted'); testing.resetBannerEmittedForTests(); if(hasEmittedCliBanner()) throw new Error('banner reset did not clear state'); emitCliBanner('2026.3.7',options); if(writes.length!==2) throw new Error('expected two writes after reset, got '+writes.length); process.stdout.write=originalWrite; console.log('ad hoc banner reset check passed');"
Evidence after fix: Console output from the real source checkout:
```
ad hoc banner reset check passed
```
Observed result after fix: The first emit set hasEmittedCliBanner() to true, resetBannerEmittedForTests() cleared it to false, and a second emit produced a second stdout write in the same module instance.
What was not tested: A packaged OpenClaw install was not launched; this PR only exposes a test reset helper and does not change production CLI banner emission behavior.

Verification:
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/banner.test.ts src/cli/channel-options.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/banner.ts src/cli/banner.test.ts
- git diff --check
